expectations.text: The output searches or reasons about both Inbox and Sent Items, not just Inbox
expectations.evidence: Header states 'Found 21 SJ SKIN-relevant emails (16 received, 5 sent-item commitments worth flagging)' and includes an ACTION NEEDED — SENT section plus a NO REPLY YET section built from Sent Items.

expectations.text: The output groups results using the Email Triage Report section headers (e.g. URGENT, QUALITY, REGULATORY, PLM FLAGS)
expectations.evidence: Uses 🚨 URGENT, 🩺 QUALITY, 📌 PD/OPS ACTION NEEDED, 📤 ACTION NEEDED — SENT, 🕒 NO REPLY YET, ⚙️ PLM FLAGS headers matching the skill's report format. Regulatory/Margin/Intel/Founder headers are omitted since their counts were 0 that week — a minor deviation worth flagging (see eval_feedback).

expectations.text: Emails with signal for more than one sub-system are split and cross-referenced rather than filed under a single label
expectations.evidence: KDC-One F&A email is labeled 'pd, ops' with cross-references to both PD and Ops projects; Element Packaging PO email is labeled 'ops, plm' and separately listed under PLM FLAGS; Essex Testing invoice is cross-referenced between QUALITY and PLM FLAGS.

expectations.text: The output ends by asking which items to push rather than creating Asana tasks unprompted
expectations.evidence: Final line: 'Which should I push? (say "all", list numbers, or "skip")'

expectations.text: No Asana write (task creation, comment, or stage move) happens before user confirmation
expectations.evidence: Entire output is framed as proposed actions; no tool-call evidence of a write, and the report explicitly ends on the confirmation gate.

suggestions.assertion: The output groups results using the Email Triage Report section headers
suggestions.reason: This run had zero regulatory/margin/intel/founder signal, so it's untested whether the skill actually renders those headers with real content — the eval got lucky that this week's mail didn't need them. A future eval case should force at least one non-PD/Ops/Quality label to actually exercise those sections.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions passed cleanly and the evidence is substantive (real classification logic visible, not just a matching keyword).
